zh.lifeSummary: 當Roy Captain White於1902年1月20日出生在美國威斯康辛州索耶史東 蕾克時，他的父親Benjamin Yerxa White II55歲，母親Effie Mae Colbroth37歲。他於1925年8月15日與Ruth Mary Covey在美國華盛頓州克拉克溫哥華結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他在美國威斯康辛州索耶辛特 蕾克住了大約5年。他去世於1936年2月22日在美國華盛頓州斯卡馬尼亞卡森享年34歲，埋葬在美國華盛頓州斯卡馬尼亞卡森笛卡爾 卡森 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roy Captain White于1902年1月20日出生在美国威斯康辛州索耶史東 蕾克时，他的父亲Benjamin Yerxa White II55岁，母亲Effie Mae Colbroth37岁。他于1925年8月15日与Ruth Mary Covey在美国华盛顿州克拉克溫哥華结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他在美国威斯康辛州索耶辛特 蕾克住了大约5年。他去世于1936年2月22日在美国华盛顿州斯卡馬尼亞卡森享年34岁，埋葬在美国华盛顿州斯卡馬尼亞卡森笛卡爾 卡森 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Roy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Scottish name, representing an Anglicized spelling of the Gaelic nickname Ruadh ‘red’. It has since spread to other parts of the English-speaking world, where it is often reanalysed as Old French roy ‘king’ ( compare Leroy ).

nameMeanings.name: Captain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Constant, Emile, Marcell.

Americanized form (translation into English) of French Capitaine, Dutch Captein or Kaptein , Italian Capitano , German and Polish or other Slavic Kapitan , Hungarian Kapitány, and Greek Kapetanos, all status names from a title meaning ‘captain’ (ultimately from Late Latin capitaneus ‘chief’, ‘principal’ from caput ‘head’), used to denote a master of a ship, a high-ranking official, a headman, or a person of military rank. In North America, the surname Captain may also be derived (shortened and translated) from various Greek patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Kapetanopoulos. Compare Capitan .

variants.name: Capitano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Sal, Angelo, Gilda, Orazio, Sarina.
Italian (Sicily and Naples): status name from capitano ‘captain’, a title denoting the master of a ship, an official of some kind, or an officer in the army. Compare Captain .

variants.name: Kapitan
meanings: From Late Latin Capito, originally a nickname meaning ‘big-headed’; the name of a missionary who preached in the Crimea and south Russia in the 4th century.

variants.name: Kaptein
meanings: Dutch: status name from kaptein, a title meaning ‘captain’. Compare Captain .

historicEvents.title: Department of Commerce and Labor
historicEvents.description: A short-lived Cabinet department which was concerned with controlling the excesses of big business. Later being split and the Secretary of Commerce and Labor splitting into two separate positions.

historicEvents.title: Pike's Market
historicEvents.description: Pike’s Market is one of the oldest still working farmer’s markets in the US. It is located in Seattle’s central business district, just north of Belltown , and southwest of central waterfront and Elliott Bay. One of the attractions there is the gum wall.

historicEvents.title: The Girl Scouts
historicEvents.description: Like the Boy Scouts of America, The Girl Scouts is a youth organization for girls in the United States. Its purpose is to prepare girls to empower themselves and by acquiring practical skills.

historicEvents.title: Boeing Aircraft
historicEvents.description: The Boeing Aircraft was named and created by William Boeing. The first two planes were Bluebill and Mallard and the first flight was July 15, 1916. Boeing aircrafts made their debut during WWI.

historicEvents.title: The President Dies of a Heart Attack
historicEvents.description: Warrant G. Harding died of a heart attack in the Palace hotel in San Francisco.
historicEvents.urlTitle: The unexpected death of President Harding

historicEvents.title: Amendment of Equal Rights
historicEvents.description: Is a proposed amendment to help guarantee equal legal rights for all citizens of the United States. Its main objective is to end legal distinctions between the two genders in terms of divorce, property, employment, and other legal matters. Even though it isn't the 28th Amendment yet, it has started conversations about the meaning of legal equality.
The Star-Spangled Banner is adopted as the national anthem.

historicEvents.title: Wisconsin Passes First Unemployment Compensation Law
historicEvents.description: The nation's first unemployment compensation law was passed in Wisconsin on January 28, 1932.

en.lifeSummary: When Roy Captain White was born on 20 January 1902, in Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United States, his father, Benjamin Yerxa White II, was 55 and his mother, Effie Mae Colbroth, was 37. He married Ruth Mary Covey on 15 August 1925, in Vancouver, Clark, Washington, United States. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He lived in Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United States for about 5 years. He died on 22 February 1936, in Carson, Skamania, Washington, United States, at the age of 34, and was buried in Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, Washington, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roy Captain White est né le 20 janvier 1902, à Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, États-Unis, son père, Benjamin Yerxa White II, avait 55 ans et sa mère, Effie Mae Colbroth, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Ruth Mary Covey le 15 août 1925, à Vancouver, Clark, Washington, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, États-Unis pendant environ 5 ans. Il est décédé le 22 février 1936, à Carson, Skamania, Washington, États-Unis, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterré à Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, Washington, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roy Captain White am 20. Januar 1902 in Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Benjamin Yerxa White II 55 und seine Mutter Effie Mae Colbroth 37. Er hat am 15. August 1925 in Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten Ruth Mary Covey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er hat ungefähr 5 Jahre lang in Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 22. Februar 1936 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Carson, Skamania, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roy Captain White è nato il il 20 gennaio 1902 nel Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Benjamin Yerxa White II, aveva 55 anni e il suo madre, Effie Mae Colbroth, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Ruth Mary Covey il 15 agosto 1925, nel Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Stati Uniti d'America per circa 5 anni. Morì il 22 febbraio 1936, nel Carson, Skamania, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolto a Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Roy Captain Whiteが1902年1月20日にアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州サウヤーストーン ラクで生まれた時，父Benjamin Yerxa White IIは55歳，母Effie Mae Colbrothは37歳であった。1925年8月15日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クラークヴァンコウバーでRuth Mary Coveyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州サウヤーサンド ラクに約5年間住んでいた。彼は1936年2月22日にアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州スカマニアカーソンで34歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ワシントン州スカマニアカーソンオルド カーソン セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United St: 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 스톤 레이크

ko.Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, W: 미국 워싱턴 주 스카먼이아 카슨 드 카슨 세멧리
ko.Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United Sta: 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 상드 레이크
ko.lifeSummary: Roy Captain White(이)가 1902년 1월 20일 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 스톤 레이크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Benjamin Yerxa White II(은)는 55세였고, 그의 어머니 Effie Mae Colbroth(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1925년 8월 15일 미국 워싱턴 주 클라르크 버코우버에서 Ruth Mary Covey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 위스콘신 주 사w예르 상드 레이크에서 약 5년 동안 살았다. 그는 1936년 2월 22일 미국 워싱턴 주 스카먼이아 카슨에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 워싱턴 주 스카먼이아 카슨 드 카슨 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roy Captain White nasceu em 20 de janeiro de 1902, em Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Benjamin Yerxa White II, tinha 55 anos e sua mãe, Effie Mae Colbroth, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Ruth Mary Covey em 15 de agosto de 1925, em Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América por quase 5 anos. Ele faleceu em 22 de fevereiro de 1936, em Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos da América, com 34 anos, e foi sepultado em Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, United Sta: сэнд леик, савиир, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roy Captain White родился 20 января 1902 г. в местности стоун леик, савиир, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Benjamin Yerxa White II было 55 лет, а матери, которую звали Effie Mae Colbroth, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Ruth Mary Covey 15 августа 1925 г. в местности ванкоувир, кларк, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности сэнд леик, савиир, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты около 5 лет. Он умер 22 февраля 1936 г. в местности карсон, скаманя, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 34 лет и был похоронен в местности олд карсон кеметири, карсон, скаманя, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roy Captain White nació el 20 de enero de 1902, en Stone Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos, su papá, Benjamin Yerxa White II, tenía 55 años y su mamá, Effie Mae Colbroth, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Ruth Mary Covey el 15 de agosto de 1925, en Vancouver, Clark, Washington, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Vivió en Sand Lake, Sawyer, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos durante unos 5 años. Murió el 22 de febrero de 1936, en Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultado en Old Carson Cemetery, Carson, Skamania, Washington, Estados Unidos.
